Transcrição Completa: How to Build a Killer Follow Up System That Converts Ghosted Leads
[00:00]
"Okay. So, one thing that's been coming up quite frequently over this last I'd say about two weeks since we've had this mastermind, um you guys have had a lot of attention on what to do with all the people that come through whatever system you've got that just are failing to close."
[00:18]
"So, if you consider this to be as an example like people who book calls maybe don't show up to the calls. Uh people who book calls show up and don't purchase. People who opt into a webinar don't show up. people who opt into a webinar do show up, don't buy. Uh things like this, okay? All your unconverted leads."
[00:38]
"I want to be very direct when I say this, okay? It's not that tough. And to be able to build systems around it is also not that tough. There's a very specific way of thinking about this that unlocks all of the thoughts and all of the systems and all the specific ways to kind of clean up those leads."
[00:56]
"And we're going to talk about them all here today. Okay? And this is a key talking talking point during Q4 that we have to put attention on to because as you guys all know costs generally go up a bit during Q4."
[01:09]
"Our margins get eaten into and sometimes that can snuff people out from advertising entirely during this period of time. So we need more ways to generate revenue besides just focusing on front end exclusively. And that's what that's a bit of what we're going to talk about here today."
[01:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Underlying Intention - Original Conversion Mechanism Failed"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados numericamente
VISUAL: Conceito central destacado - quando leads não convertem, o sistema original falhou, não necessariamente o lead
[01:27]
"So let me get started with this. Okay. The underlying intention that you have to understand that causes somebody to fail to convert is the original conversion mechanism failed. Okay? Whatever it is that you originally put them through, it failed to do its job."
[01:44]
"Okay? If you look at it like this, you have a much higher probability to unlock the thinking that we're about to talk about. Okay? So, let me just give you a clear example."
[01:54] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Factory Conveyor Belt Analogy"
NÚMEROS: 50% dos produtos não chegam ao final
VISUAL: Comparação visual de factory/conveyor belt - produtos caindo antes do fim da linha de produção, representando leads perdidos
[01:56]
"If I have somebody that books a call through a standard call funnel, as an example, okay, and they fail to show up to the call, the system failed. Okay? It's that fucking simple. The system failed."
[02:09]
"It's like if we ran a factory and we had a conveyor belt operating system that was meant to produce products and it only got 50% of the products to the other side of the conveyor belt. As a factory operator, the last fucking thing we would do is just say, 'Well, let's just let's just focus on um you know, the ones that get to the other side of the conveyor belt exclusively.'"
[02:30]
"Obviously, we'd put attention onto the 50% that aren't making it to the other side of the fucking production line so we could sell it. Okay, so again, from that perspective, it's the same thing with all funnels."
[02:42]
"If we get somebody to opt into a webinar, they fail to show up to the webinar, the mechanism failed. If we get somebody to opt into a webinar, they do show up, they fail to convert on the webinar, and the mechanism failed."
[02:54] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Little Things - Easy to Fix Problems"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados numericamente
VISUAL: Lista de pequenos problemas: scheduling conflicts, trust issues, AI search manipulations, multiple webinar viewers
[02:56]
"Now, to be fair, sometimes there's like little things, okay? And so, let me give you some examples of the little things so you guys can understand that. The little things would be as an example like scheduling conflicts, you know, they had something come up."
[03:11]
"Uh, depending on the demographic you sell to, obviously this can range in the excuse type that they can provide to you. This could be, you know, oh, my kids had some kind of emergency, my dog had an emergency, you know, some kind of opportunity that was a higher priority came up compared to this."
[03:27]
"Whatever it is, all right, scheduling conflict could be a simple thing. Um, sometimes it's a trust issue. And so, as you guys know, with that with that fifth backend selling system, the AI manipulations specifically, we talked about, you know, somebody could do something as simple as just say, 'Hey, uh, is this company legit? Is this company a scam? Is this company reviews?'"
[03:48]
"And instead of actually reviewing anything anymore, most people just read the AI search overview, and if you're not fucking green lit by the AI gods, you're fucking cooked. Okay? Sometimes it could be something like that."
[04:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Multiple Webinar Viewers"
NÚMEROS: 1/3 (um terço) dos compradores assistem pelo menos 2 webinars antes de comprar
VISUAL: Estatística destacada mostrando padrão de comportamento de compra através de múltiplas exposições
[04:02]
"In other instances like we have clients where they see without exaggeration one-third of their total buyers from webinars that watch at least two webinars before they buy. Okay? So sometimes it could be things like that."
[04:15]
"There's all kinds of little things, okay, that can fuck you and be justified as easy to fix problems. Okay? Most of these little things, they don't require much at all. They require you to have like a setter team as an examiner."
[04:27]
"I can reach back out to these people and just try to re-engage. Hey, that call time didn't work. No problem. Is tomorrow afternoon or in the evening better for you? Or do you want to do it in the morning on Friday? Right? It's like that could be just the littlest thing that helps solve and capture a lot of that revenue again."
[04:44]
"And I'm sure I'm sure majority of you guys are doing that. marketing automation sequences in place for cleanup, reinvitation sequences, right? And then obviously from the trust issue perspective, we have all the backend selling systems. We have all five of them that we can exploit to try to increase the trust and therefore increase those statistics of the back end."
[05:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Bigger Things - Accumulated Unsold Leads"
NÚMEROS: $25,000-$75,000 por webinar, $15-$25 custo por lead, milhares de pessoas por webinar, dezenas de vendas
VISUAL: Case study de cliente com webinars semanais durante 6 meses, escalando investimento progressivamente
[05:07]
"But now let me let me be clear on where I'm really coming from with some of the bigger things. Okay? So bigger things are accumulated unsold leads. So as an example of this, we had a client uh working with them right now. They've been doing webinars for about six months weekly. Okay?"
[05:24]
"And every single webinar, they spent $25,000 minimum. They've spent upwards of $50,000 on each one of these most recent webinars. Some of them as high as $75,000 as they've scaled. Okay."
[05:37]
"Now, every single webinar they produce generally about a 15 to maybe upwards of $25 on the costly ones for a cost per lead. They're getting low thousands of people per webinar and they're selling dozens of these people. Okay."
[05:52] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Interest Spectrum"
NÚMEROS: 4 níveis de interesse
VISUAL: Escala vertical mostrando 4 níveis: Curiosity (base) → General Interest → Highly Interested → Convicted (topo). Linha de conversão acima do nível "Convicted"
[05:54]
"Now, as you guys know, we believe deeply in the interest in the interest spectrum here where we know with certainty there's four levels of interest. Okay? We got the curiosity level. We have general interest. We have highly interested. And then at the top we have convicted. Okay."
[06:12]
"And for those that have been around this system long enough, you guys know deals happen above the conviction mode. Okay. And simply put, like if we get a bunch of people that opt in for webinars, thousands that remain unsold, there's obviously a high probability that they remain in the curious or general interest level."
[06:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Interest Drops Over Time"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados numericamente
VISUAL: Gráfico descendente mostrando interesse caindo com o tempo, com pequenos picos quando há intervenções (vídeos, calls, webinars)
[06:32]
"Now in the concept of the interest spectrum the other thing that we have to understand is is that as time passes interest goes down. Okay. So it's it's like this. It just falls slowly as we incrementally move through time."
[06:47]
"And the only thing that bumps it up are the random things that we do like having a key video and they hammer them reach them, a setter conversation, another webinar, another opportunity to convert through a different funnel, right?"
[07:00]
"Whatever it is, interest drops, okay, as time passes until that next thing from us gets in front of them. That's effective, right? It's not just about getting whatever in front of them. It's obviously about getting the right thing in front of them."
[07:14]
"And so from that perspective, when you go back to the original intention here where you think of all this from the perspective of, okay, if I'm getting a fuckload of people, thousands, right? Thousands in this example of unsold leads and I consider the perspective that my original conversion mechanism in this particular example a webinar has failed to convert thousands and thousands of individuals."
[07:37]
"Why would I not pay attention to those thousands and thousands of individuals and rotate how I am attempting to sell to them? Okay, one of my all-time favorite authors, his name is Richard Bandler."
[07:49] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Richard Bandler - VAK System"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados numericamente
VISUAL: Conceito NLP (Neuro-Linguistic Programming), livro "Frogs into Princes", sistema VAK (Visual, Auditory, Kinesthetic)
[07:51]
"Okay, now Richard Bandler, he technically conceptualized NLP, the neural linguistic programming, taught guys like Tony Robbins. Richard Bandler has some amazing books, arguably my favorite author of all time. I hope to meet Richard Banler again before he dies. Uh, getting a little older now. Okay."
[08:09]
"Anyway, long story short, Richard Banler has this concept in this book and it's called Frogs into Princes. Okay. And in this in this book, Frogs into Princes, an incredible read, okay? He introduces essentially neural linguistic programming at an event that he transcribed into a book."
[08:26]
"And there's and there's one particular page where he talks about this system called the VAK system. Right? Now, VAK stands for visual auditory kinesthetic."
[08:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Rotate Communication Until It Lands"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados numericamente
VISUAL: Conceito de rotação de terapias: hipnoterapia → exercícios visuais → exercícios auditivos → exercícios kinestésicos, até encontrar o que funciona
[08:37]
"And the whole concept of what I'm trying to tell you right now is he essentially says because he teaches psychotherapists for a living. He's telling them in this book when he introduces this NLP VAK, visual auditory canesthetic system, he's like, 'Look, if you attempt to communicate something to an individual and you fail to make your point or you fail to have the effectiveness of the therapy, you rotate to a different therapy mechanism in until you get the result in the outcome."
[09:04]
"So in this example, like he'll rotate through hypnotherapy, he'll rotate through a visual exercise, he'll rotate through an auditory exercise, he'll rotate through kinesesthetic exercises until he finds the one that lands. Okay?"
[09:17]
"In his world, the patient never leaves in the state that they came in that troubled state that they are paying to get away from. Okay? No. By comparison, he believes as every as every therapist should believe that people are paying for outcomes, not for time and effort."
[09:34]
"He doesn't want people to stay with him forever. He just wants ideally get them in and out in a session and solve their problem. So, he's optimized for outcomes and results. From that perspective, okay, what you have to understand is is you rotate how you communicate something until it lands. Okay?"
[09:53] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Single Conversion Mechanism Business Problem"
NÚMEROS: 1 dia por semana, 1 horário, closers disponíveis 10 AM - 6 PM EST
VISUAL: Limitações destacadas - webinar único semanal, horário fixo, vendedores apenas em horário comercial, vendendo para pessoas que trabalham nesses horários
[09:55]
"So, as an example, like there's some of you that sit here where you are a single conversion mechanism business, right? Like the client that I'm giving the example of, well, they only had webinars. They only had that one fucking way to convert."
[10:09]
"And guess what? They only had one fucking day of the week with one time every day of the week to watch that said webinar. Okay? And guess what? The scheduling of the closers was also the same for every day of the week."
[10:22]
"They had closers available between about 10:00 a.m. Eastern Standard Time until the latest, about 6:00 p.m. Eastern Standard Time. Guess who they sold to? People who work during those fucking times. Okay? Made no sense."
[10:35]
"They have to introduce variables that are outside of their current norm. Aka, they have to introduce alternative conversion mechanisms to be able to sell and craft an incredibly well optimized follow-up system that gets all these people that failed to close in the original way."

[12:04] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Salesperson Name Test Results"
NÚMEROS: 10-syllable name: 40% show rate → David: 70% show rate → Changed to John: 60% show rate
VISUAL: Comparação de show rates baseada em simplicidade do nome do vendedor
[12:06]
"He ran this test in the most recent two months if I'm not mistaken. He had a sales rep with like a 10 syllable name. He had a sales rep named David. David's show rate was 70%. The sales rep with the 10 syllable name had a 40% show rate."
[12:22]
"He changed the sales rep name who had the 10 syllable name to I like John or something super simple like that and show rate went up to 60% straight away with just that one little change. Right? So it's like little thing. Again, just little tiny things can sometimes make all the difference."

[14:36]
"Your sales people don't have nearly the same authority as you. They aren't nearly as great of sales people in almost all instances. You put them in front of you once a week. And you could you could do this twice a week, like I said, so it's a different day and a different time. Okay?"
[14:52]

[16:21]

Costs half as much. no one-on-one calls, no in-person mastermind access, but again, it's another opportunity for people who still want access to all the stuff like this to get in here with us, which we've already had a few people take advantage of, by the way."
[17:30]

[18:13] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Cleaner Role"
NÚMEROS: 2 semanas a 1 mês de período antes de passar para cleaner
VISUAL: Estrutura de sales team com role específico para leads não convertidos, ideal para novos reps provarem valor
[18:15]
"And sometimes, by the way, this is just having salespeople, right? If you guys have ever taken my sales training course that you all have access to inside of your course libraries, we have what we call the cleaner roll. Okay?"
[18:28]
"Cleaner roles absolutely rip at scale, especially, it's a great place for a new rep who joins into the organization to kind of prove themselves out. You after a certain amount of time of a lead being unclosed, and this could be two weeks, this could be a month, you know, it's dependent on your system and how long your average sales cycle is."
[18:48]
"Once they drop out of the process where you know with certainty that your main salespeople who were originally signed that lead, they're going to do fucking all with that person, right? Hand that lead off to a cleaner. Okay?"
[19:01]
"Get them in the hands of somebody whose sole job it is to take the leads that are left unclosed and continue following up with them and load that person up with assets like what we've talked about here."
[19:12]
"Give them trust and sales assets. Give them content to send out. Give them webinar recordings. Give them opportunities to invite people to owner calls. Give them opportunities to invite people to webinars."

[19:39]
"They have the front-end mechanisms that are the most profitable that they keep on the front end and typically spend the most money on, but more importantly, they have a handful of these backend cleanup tools to maximize the amount of revenue that comes in from all these people that are left unsold. [23:33]

[23:40]
"Yeah, absolutely. Yeah. Thank you."
[23:43]
"Yeah, we we we love them a lot. Um, Jamie, you want to unmute yourself and go over what it is that you do or give any kind of lesson on that if you care to. All good. If not."
[23:54]
"Yeah. And I don't know if we're doing it as well as as you guys do. I think for a lot of us um on our Q&A's, we're also just kind of like telling them where to focus and kind of just yelling at them and being like, 'Make a fucking decision. Get off the fence and run.'"
[24:10]
"It's just like for me, I'm just trying to drive urgency into them to get off the fence. So, mostly I just yell at them and it's also kind of like a framing thing so that they see me as the owner yelling at people and they're like, 'Oh fucking this is this is legit.' you know that works really well."
[24:27]
"Nice. Very good. Okay, cool. Thank you for that. So, long story short, just to be just to be clear on what we talked about so far, okay, remember when you look at this whole concept of how can I build a follow-up system that converts all my ghosted leads, the way to look at it is is whatever they originally opted into that failed to convert them, I should try something else instead. Okay?"
[24:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Daniel Fazio - List Kit Success"
NÚMEROS: $1M+ por mês, múltiplas ofertas, horários 6 PM - 11 PM e manhã cedo com show rates mais altos
VISUAL: Lições chave: múltiplas ofertas, scheduling além do 9-5, horários off-hours para workers
[24:52]
"And sometimes those are little things like sometimes it's expanding the schedule. One of one of my favorite uh inner circle members in terms of his talk that he gave on stage was Daniel Fazio. Dan Daniel Fosio runs list kit. He gave him a million-doll month trophy."
[25:08]
"During that specific talk for his mastermind talk, he he gave a whole bunch of great advice, but one of the specific things that I really like that he said was about his call funnel because that was a big part of how he got to his million dollar a month."
[25:22]
"He talked about a few things. Number one, he said you should have a fuckload of different offers for people. That way you can convert a lot more people than you otherwise would if you only had one offer. And that reiterates that lesson I've already shared with you here today."
[25:35]
"But he also talked about scheduling, right? He was like, stop having such a traditional window. You know, he gave the example like, oh, if you sell to 9 to 5 people, like why would you have a fucking schedule that only allows people to book between 9:00 and 5, right?"
[25:51]
"He he talked about having off hours like between 6:00 p.m. and like 11:00 p.m. as an example, or very early in the morning. Those were some of the times that they were able to quantify. they had higher show rates. Okay."
[26:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Webinar Show Rate Optimization"
NÚMEROS: Highest show rate: Monday opt-ins, 2nd: Sunday, 3rd: Saturday, 4th: Friday
VISUAL: Timing strategy - quanto mais próximo da data do webinar o opt-in acontece, maior o show rate
[26:07]
"Um to be clear, sometimes little things, by the way, like let's use the example of a webinar. We have a client webinar coming up on Monday. Today's Saturday. I'll launch the ads for that yesterday night, late into the night. Why?"
[26:21]
"Because the highest show rate is the people who opt in on Monday. The second highest show rate comes from people who opt in on Sunday. The third highest show rate comes from people who opt in today. And the fourth highest show rate would be people who opted in yesterday."
[26:35]
"So, the more money that I can spend closer to the actual webinar start date, the higher my show r's going to be and therefore the higher quantity of people that I'm probable to close. It's like remember little things, you absolutely want to be thinking with the little things, okay?"
[26:51] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Bigger Things Summary"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados numericamente
VISUAL: Lista completa: accumulated unsold leads, interest dropped, alternative funnel mechanisms, call funnels, webinars, low ticket, alternative offers, cleaner role, weekly/bi-weekly owner cleanup calls
[26:53]
"And then you get into bigger things, okay? Bigger things, remember, you typically have, especially as time passes and more money gets spent, a higher quantity of these accumulated unsold leads and interest has dropped."
[27:05]
"So you have to come at it from the perspective of how can I raise this person's interest. Again, I need alternative funnel mechanisms for that. Like if I'm running a webinar, I'm going to introduce a call funnel on the back end for retargeting."
[27:18]
"I'm going to introduce low ticket. I'm going to introduce alternative offers. I'm going to make sure that I have a cleaner role in my sales organization. I'm going to make sure that I do the once a week or eventually twice a week owner follow-up cleanup calls. Right? Those are some examples of bigger things. Okay?"

[27:37]

[27:53]

[28:06]

[28:28]

[28:45]
"It's one of my highest productive times of the year in terms of like work I can get done. So this concept of the for the dogs email sequence during a window like that is to communicate directly to that type of person in your marketing messaging."
[29:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "For The Dogs Email Structure"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados numericamente
VISUAL: Subject line "for the dogs", mensagem targeting pessoas trabalhando durante feriados, oferta especial com incentivo adicional para ação imediata
[29:02]
"So you adapt the back-end messaging with a simple email sequence that says subject line for the dogs. Okay? And you can change that to your own words like you know as an example. I'm sure some of you sitting here would never say for the dogs. You'd probably say something like for the hungry person working right now or something like that. Right?"
[29:21]
"Moral of the story, I digress, is the email itself is essentially saying if you are one of those few people during this time of the year that is actively trying to get better, earn more, and evolve, I've got an opportunity for you. Here it is."
[29:36]
"And as a matter of fact, you take action on it. Between this window right here, I'm going to give you X. And that could be an additional thing. It could be a discount. whatever it is you want it to be, you are rewarding the behavior of getting a response during a period of time where people are typically highly unresponsive. Okay, so as an example of like cleanup mechanisms, we have these tactics that we can start to get into like the for the dogs email sequence."
[30:01] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Sent From iPhone Email Tactic"
NÚMEROS: Centenas de milhares enviados, 40,000+ respostas no primeiro dia, 48 horas de shutdown da empresa
VISUAL: Email de Grant Cardone - subject "following up" (lowercase), one-liner "are you still interested in sales training?", assinatura "- grant", "sent from my iPhone" footer
[30:03]
"Additional tactics. Okay, when I this is this is a long time ago at this point. I I actually don't even know the amount of years it's been. It's probably been like 13 years um since may no actually maybe like 12 years since I worked at Mr. Grant Cardone's office."
[30:20]
"And I got in big fucking trouble for this specific email that we sent. Okay, it was the sent from iPhone email. Okay. And what we did on this one is we had an email list of about I don't know a couple hundred thou high couple hundred thousand people at this time."
[30:38]
"And I I just wanted to try something out. Okay, I did a all lowercase subject line. It said following up. Okay, just all lowercase following up. I made it look like it was sent from Grant, right? So, it's it's sent from the owner in this case as the tactic."
[30:56]
"And it was a simple one-liner email that said, 'Are you still interested in sales training?' Right? I I can't remember if I specifically said, 'Are you still interested in sales training?' Or if I said, 'Are you still interested in?' And then the product name question mark."
[31:13]
"And it was all lowercase. It was not punctu grammatically correct. Like I didn't use proper punctuation or sentence casing. I just said all lowercase subject line. All lowercase following up that one liner. Are you still interested in sales training? All lowercase with the question mark."
[31:31]
"And then I entered down and I put dash grant. And then below that, I entered down and I copied the standard sent from my iPhone. That would make it look like it was sent from an iPhone."
[31:43]
"And then from there, I pushed the bottom part of the email down by pressing enter a bunch of times. So technically, the address and the opt out thing and the unsubscribe thing were all still there. To be clear, you obviously can't take that out legally, but we pushed it down and I sent it to the entire list."
[32:01]
"I honestly thought the COO was going to beat the fucking out of me with the response that we got from this. Okay, she was so mad at me. Um, I didn't expect this to work to the level that it did. out of the high hundreds of thousands of people that we emailed, we had without exaggeration like 40 plus thousand responses within the first day, if not more than that."
[32:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Emergency Response Protocol"
NÚMEROS: 40,000+ respostas, 48 horas, todos os departamentos paralisados
VISUAL: Operação de emergência - accounting (4 pessoas), real estate, shipping, creative team, designers, todos forwardando emails para sales reps por 48 horas
[32:27]
"It was such an extreme amount, just to put it in perspective, that every single individual department from the accounting team, which is like four accountants, um, the real estate guy at the time name was Tyler, every single fucking per like the shipping department, everybody in my creative team that we had there, like the the graphic designers and the web, everybody had to stop what they were doing, log into this email."
[32:52]
"They were assigned a specific set of pages and they had to open every single email and forward it to a specific set of sales reps that they were assigned to. Okay? And the amount of sales activity that came from that, I had no idea how much revenue came from that."
[33:07]
"I just know they had to shut down the company for like fucking 48 hours. And this was a random reactive thing that I put the company into, which is why I say the COO, I think, wanted to honestly kick the fucking out of me."
[33:21]
"But I digress. My point being the send from my iPhone email is an example of one of those tactics where you revitalize the interest of dead leads. Okay, it's like a another classic example of this without having to do the send from my iPhone email is the is this dead? Okay, so we we technically use this as like the where where did we lose you?"
[33:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Where Did We Lose You Email"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados numericamente
VISUAL: Email curto e direto - "wondering if I can get quick one sentence feedback on where we lost you", 2 tipos de respostas: feedback real OU "you didn't lose me" + justificativa
[33:47]
"That's the actual like communication that we use in this in this is this dead style tactic. Okay. So, we'll send out we'll send out a s if enough time passed and the person's ghosting us. Okay."
[34:00]
"And we we've done this with people as an example who have gotten an agreement sent to them as an agency and then all of a sudden they just like stop talking to us. Okay. Or to somebody who's been highly responsive then again all of a sudden they just start they just stop talking to us. Okay."
[34:15]
"Or you can use this obviously for every single scenario I've already articulated above. Somebody came through a call funnel, didn't show up, showed up, didn't buy, didn't show up to a webinar, did show up to a webinar, didn't buy."
[34:27]
"Whatever it is, you send a simple email that just says, 'Wondering if I can get a quick one sentence feedback from you on where we lost you. Appreciate you. Please let me know.' Okay, now that simple simple like two sentence email wondering where we lost you would appreciate your feedback. Thank you. Right?"
[34:46]
"Typically what you get back as a response is one of two things. You either get back actual feedback on where you lost them or in most instances which is the most interesting thing of this email you actually get the person to say, 'Oh no, you didn't lose me.'"
[35:02]
"and then they make some kind of justification or reason for why they've been ghosting you and then they all of a sudden are back in the buying process. Okay, so a majority of the time with that specific tactic, we revitalize the lead and we essentially get them to be self-aware that they've been giving us that impression that we lost the deal, right?"
[35:22]
"And then they all of a sudden say, 'Oh, no, you didn't lose the deal.' Like, 'I'm sorry.' And then they explain themselves. Okay? Okay, so my point is is like when you look at little tactics like these like the fur the dogs email sequence which is great during a period of time like as an example during Thanksgiving weekend when everybody's going to be in like a Black Friday frenzy during that little window between the holidays and the new year right before the dogs email sequence rips during that period of time."
[35:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Break The Glass Tactics"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados numericamente
VISUAL: Táticas de emergência para spike de revenue: Sent From iPhone email, Where Did We Lose You email - revitalizam dead leads imediatamente
[35:52]
"The sent from my iPhone email, we call these break the glass tactics. Okay, this is where if we want a big old spike of revenue and we want to revitalize a bunch of dead leads, we'll break out tactics like the sent from my iPhone email and the where did we lose you email."
[36:07]
"I'm not exaggerating. Y'all can right after you hang up from this call, go into your email provider, do the sent from my iPhone email or do the where did we lose you email. I personally like the where did we lose you email better. It fucking rips. Okay, it rips. you get a bunch of people that are immediately responsive. Okay?"

Okay?"
[36:53]
"And what this reveals, they'll either tell you to go fucking yourself because you just blasted them literally everywhere, or they'll be like, 'This is the best place to communicate with me.' And now you actually know the best place to communicate."
[37:06]

[37:23]

[38:10]

[38:23] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "iOS 17 Update Communication Changes"

VISUAL: Mudanças na deliverability: texts menos efetivos, filtros ocultos, voicemail difícil de encontrar, similar a email providers agora
[38:25]

[38:42]
"When you go sign up with a personal email, all the email providers know it's a personal email. There's a higher probability that if you send a personal email to somebody that it actually lands in their inbox because they know that it's from a person."
[38:55]
"If I go sign up with a business email and I also send it from a CRM or any email deliverability provider for businesses, the email providers, meaning like Gmail, I don't even know if anybody uses any other ones anymore, but whatever, you know, if you do all those, moral of the story is they know that it's from a CRM or they know it's from an email deliverability tool and then they scrutinize you completely differently on your ability to reach that inbox."
[39:21]
"It's now the same thing with cell phone providers, okay? The people blasting out dick pill ads and all the political people. I I'll say confidently, I think it's more the political stuff than anything else that ruined this. They just blasted the fucking out of people."
[39:37]
"And as a result of that, the government had to step in and say, 'What the fucking is going on?' They put pressure on the phone companies. [39:54]

I have no idea where my voicemail is in the new iOS update, right? I think they just nuked it. I'm sure it exists, but I have no idea where it is."
[40:13]

[40:31]

[41:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "When To Use Tactics vs Systems"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados numericamente
VISUAL: Distinção clara - Little Things & Big Things = systematize/normalize, Tactics = juice tactics para slumps, não abusar
[41:40]
"Okay, so moral of the story, when it comes to like this tactical perspective, right, you have a few things. You've got little things you could test like scheduling changes. You've got big things that you can change like introducing new funnels specifically to the back end, introducing new offers, right? Introducing alternatives to what you already sell. Okay?"
[42:01]
"And in addition to that, you have tactics. You have a handful of things. Of course, there's a few more, but to be clear, you have a handful of things that you can go do tactically to try to revitalize the list, okay? To try to sell to these unsold leads."
[42:16]
"Now, the big question is is how frequently do you put attention on this and do you systematize it or do you just do a very simple manual push of certain things like this? Okay. So, and it really depends on where you're at."
[42:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Inner Circle Example - When NOT To Use Tactics"
NÚMEROS: $10K/mês, anúncio apenas em Instagram Stories
VISUAL: Exemplo de quando NÃO usar tactics - já há excesso de demanda, price hike anunciado minimamente para controlar fluxo
[42:32]
"I'll give you a perfect example. The literal last thing that I would do right now for the inner circle is one of these specific tactics because it would add far more people to an already rushed quantity of people. There's a high quantity of people buying this fucking"
[42:49]
"If I tried any of these specific tactics I just articulated and added even more people into the pipeline, I talked about our price hike in one place in one place alone. Insta stories, which is honestly one of the one of the smallest viewed places that I could say it. Okay."
[43:07]
"Why? Well, I want to be fair. You know, usually people who watch my Insta stories are very in tune to like the things I do. They pay the most attention in my opinion comparatively to all the other content that I put out. They're also very consistently watching."
[43:23]
"So, as a result of that, I was like, 'Hey, I'll be fair and I'll say like, 'Hey, we got the price hike coming.' Okay, I'm not putting that out on fucking YouTube. I'm not tweeting that. I'm not making like a standard post about that."
[43:35]
"And god forbid am I not emailing that out because I'd create a fury of people that want to get into it. And it's the exact opposite of what we want. It's exactly why we're raising the price, trying to slow it down. Okay?"
[43:47] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "When TO Use Tactics - Scaled Offers"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados numericamente
VISUAL: Master Internet Marketing Program, Half Circle - scaled offers que podem usar tactics quando: calendário de vendas está light, revenue em slump, quer juice
[43:49]
"And that's my point is you have to be mindful of when you do the tactic. Now, let me now let me give you the exact opposite of a comparison, okay? I can sell the master internet marketing program any fucking day of the week, all day, every day. I can sell technically the half circle now all day, every day, right?"
[44:08]
"And will I send through these tactics messaging about the half circle or messaging about master internet marketing? Fucking yeah. Fucking yeah. Those are scaled offers right now. When is the best time to do it? When your sales team's calendar's light. when your revenue hit that little slump and you want to juice it a bit. Okay, these are like juice tactics, okay?"
[44:30]
"It's like pulling out the needle, okay? Putting it in the butt, getting a little bit of juice in you, okay? That's what these specific tactics are. These are not things that you want to habitualize abusing, okay?"
[44:43]
"You ideally pull out these specific lists of tactics during periods of times where you're naturally going to have some slump activity going on and you want to immediately get out of the slump and you want to create an excess of sales activity."
[44:57]
"Why did the COO want to beat the fucking out of me when I did the send for my iPhone email? Cuz we were rocking and rolling. There was nothing wrong. There was no slump. We had a lot of momentum."
[45:08]
"Technically, I interfered with the momentum that we already had by making every single department in the company log into an email and send it over to the salespeople for 48 full hours."
[45:20]
"Technically, I interfered with the sales team's existing activity and momentum and pipeline management by introducing 40,000 new people into it within a 48 hour period of time. Right? We got responses from that email for without exaggeration upwards of two weeks. And it also showed us a lot about how people check their emails. Okay, my point is is like you don't want to do these things when you already have an exceptional amount of momentum."
[45:46]
"You ideally do these things when the tactics specifically when you need to create the additional momentum. Okay."
[45:53] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Systematize vs Tactical Approach"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados numericamente
VISUAL: SYSTEMATIZE: little things, big things, cleaner roles, weekly owner calls, multiple funnels, multiple offers. TACTICS: use during slumps, juice tactics, break glass emergencies
[45:55]
"Now, these things specifically when you look at the small things like these little things and the big things, these I think you systematize. These I think you normalize. These I think you should consistently work into day-to-day operations."
[46:08]
"Like I think every one of you should have a cleaner role. I think every one of you should be doing weekly owner cleanup calls. I think every one of you should not be like one funnel dependent. I think all of you should have multiple iterations of different funnels that you give the opportunity for people to convert through. Okay?"
[46:26] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Additional Conversion Mechanisms"
NÚMEROS: 3-day virtual events = couple million dollars cada, eventos mensais ou a cada 45 dias
VISUAL: Expansão de mecanismos: in-person events, 3-day virtual events, exemplo Cody Sanchez - múltiplas ofertas, múltiplos eventos, não é one-legged stool
[46:28]
"And this expands by the way in terms of like all these little arrows. We could have as an example of this in addition to the ones we've already written down like in-person events, right? We could write down things like 3-day virtual events on brands that we work with."
[46:43]
"Like I always I always like to point out Cody Sanchez is a great example of this. She's not a she's not a one stool business. You're not a one-legged stool. Okay. She does in-person events. She done like three in-person events. I didn't I honestly I honestly think she does in-person event regularly at this point like once a month or like at the very least like once every 45 days."
[47:04]
"She does big ones. She does smaller ones, right? She does three-day virtual events twice a year. Those create couple million-dollar lump sums every time. The only thing she doesn't do out of this list, she doesn't do the weekly owner cleanup calls, but she does webinars. She does call funnels."
[47:21]
"She also has alternative offers, right? She's got offers for existing businesses that are trying to scale through acquisitions. She's got offers for people that still work jobs and want to buy their first business. She's got offers for business coaching now. You know, she got all kinds of offers."
[47:37]
"She has a very well-developed sales team because Josh Troy and his team runs it. They run it very well. And to be clear, they have those cleaner roles. Like they have a majority of this. Again, the only thing that she doesn't have is the weekly owner cleanup call, but she'd benefit a lot from doing that if she did. So, okay, point being, I think this type of stuff should be a system that you do immediately put into action."
[48:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Leaky Bucket Analogy"
NÚMEROS: Small percentage captured vs massive hole losing everything else
VISUAL: Bucket visual com enorme buraco - maioria do que entra sai pelo outro lado, pequena porcentagem capturada, resto desperdiçado na lista morta
[48:02]
"And I want you to go and I want you to look for essentially leaks. Okay? It's the classic leaky bucket analogy, except here's the thing, right? If you actually looked at it like a bucket, okay, you have a massive fucking hole in your bucket and in reality, a majority of what you put into it just goes straight out the other side. Okay?"
[48:24]
"The only water that you technically accumulate is like a small total percentage of the people that come through this whole thing. That's all that's all you actually capture right now. Okay? All this other fucking money just pours out the other side of it."
[48:37]
"And you have no system down here in this area to actually capture any of that revenue. It all just goes to waste and just sits in your list. And guess what? Leads die when their interest level goes to zero."
[48:50]
"It's like generating a lead all over again from the very beginning if you let interest fall to a literal zero. Okay? You have to understand this. You need to fortify the bucket. Okay? Because right now, it's more like a an actual funnel. Like something comes in one side and just fucking rips out the other. Okay?"
[49:09]
"Very good. Does anybody have questions on this? Things you want to add? Anything that's working for y'all that you think you want you want to contribute to this? Feel free to unmute yourself. Fire away. Throw it in the chat if you don't want to talk. Going once. going twice. Okay, cool."

[49:29]

[49:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Andromeda Update Issues"
NÚMEROS: 5%+ link click-through rate como red flag
VISUAL: Bot traffic problem com Andromeda - high link CTR mas zero conversões, tool recomendado: Click Cease
[49:42]
"Andromeda is doing a bunch of crazy stuff. We have one thing specifically I want to pepper in just as kind of a random thing in this call to make sure that you guys go do it. We're seeing some crazy fucking right now with this Andromeda update."
[49:56]
"It looks good, but it's actually bad with having really high link clickthrough rates. We were just talking about this in the chat using tools like click like click cease. There's other tools that you can use that have similar functionality, but simply put like if you see a campaign get as high as like five plus% link click-through rate, especially if it's like random and your creatives have historically never done that and the creatives that are doing it are very similar to the ones that have never done that."
[50:26]
"um realize that we believe right now we don't think that this is malicious from competitors. You know, there's a probability of that, of course, but we don't believe so. We believe there's a higher probability that Facebook right now just has they they've always had bots. They've always had an excessive amount of bot traffic."
[50:45]
"Um, we believe that with historical updates with their with their retrieval and ranking system, which is now Andromeda and Metal Lattis, that they've had this issue dialed in and that they've prevented ads from like reaching bots and large swaths of them at that."
[51:02]
"Um, Andromedan is doing a phenomenal job at taking, you know, as we talked about before, very niche hooks and putting them in front of people who technically have a very high probability to convert on that niche hook."
[51:14]
"And unfortunately, we're we're seeing an occasional instance. It's happening without exaggeration probably about 30% of the time where we'll get somebody that says, 'Oh, I have like really high link clickthrough rates. You know, like my I'm getting like tens of thousands of people to my page."
[51:31]
"uh this is like the most efficient that my ads have ever delivered and their page conversion rates are a dumpster fire or in some instances like literally nobody even converts on the page itself out of tens of thousands of people."
[51:44]
"If that happens I'd absolutely encourage you install a tool like click cease. I would obviously not encourage it reactively. I'd encourage it proactively. We don't make a dollar obviously if you sign up with click cease. We don't give a fucking if you if if you actually use that tool or a different tool."
[52:02]
"um we've just proactively installed tools like that um into our client accounts because the liability of it you get refunded in the instance that that occurs to be clear and it also blocks those people from hitting pixels. Okay."
[52:17]
"Um can SMS verification present prevent excess bot traffic? I mean technically on lead forms yeah but I'm saying like if you run traffic through a direct response campaign, a conversion outcome campaign as an example to a page like they're they're not going to convert anyway. is going to click to the page and then it just fucking nukes your pixel."
[52:38]
"Tools like click cease would prevent it from hitting your pixel in its entirety. Uh just keep that in mind. That's that's like one very interesting Q4 issue so far."
[52:48] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Meta AI Creative Tools Warning"
NÚMEROS: Q4 2024
VISUAL: Meta AI tools em fase "dog shit" - related media, site links, dynamic destination URLs, todos problemáticos e ligados por default
[52:50]
"And then last last thing I just want to mention on Q4 is holy fucking are these meta AI creative things absolutely terrible. uh they are so bad in terms of they generally this always happens when they roll something out it's absolute dog fucking and then as time passes it gets better and better right now it is in the dog fucking phase okay"
[53:12]
"I'm talking about all the meta AI creative tools um they've as an example like site links uh related media is arguably the most problematic one in the most recent window you'll have creative as an example promoting a webinar and if you don't if you aren't mindful when you're actually on the ad level creat creating everything."
[53:33]
"It'll have this little option. Most of these are just by default turned on unless you manually turn them off. It'll by default start pulling creative from other ad campaigns that you've already run or other posts or use its generative AI to pull these different things and it'll just use random creatives with a campaign that it has no outcome for."
[53:54]
"There was a new one that they added, it seems like in this most recent few weeks where they've added a little function for destination URL being dynamically updated to some other random fucking that they might think is better, which is essentially a new manipulative way to get around you turning off site links."
[54:12] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "How To Turn Off Meta AI Features"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados numericamente
VISUAL: Step-by-step visual - Advertiser Settings → Creative Features → Uncheck box → Edit Tests → Manually uncheck all, também verificar ad level
[54:14]
"Site links, to be clear, in an ad when you when you look at the ad itself, right, site links would be these things that pop up down here. They show up as like little cubes right now. And these are all just links to different pages that you have on your site. So you can you can turn off site links easily."
[54:31]
"But the interesting thing is is like the button that actually clicks to the destination URL. They've been rotating that most recently. So when you actually add your destination URL, by the way, sometimes it takes a few seconds for this to show up."
[54:45]
"It's the most audacious fucking that I think I've seen from them in like a decade. Okay. They are they're trying some nauseating fucking on accounts right now. And I can't tell if it's just on us with high spend accounts, but I've seen it on low spend accounts, too."
[55:02]
"So, I'm just trying to blare the horn here and tell you to be mindful of this. Um, make sure you turn off all this fucking If you go into an ad account and you look at the uh the related, excuse me, if you go to an ad account and you specifically navigate to advertiser settings, um, to be clear, you can find advertiser settings very easily."
[55:25]
"Uh, let me show you guys so you can see what I'm talking about just so you're all mindful of it. on the left-hand side here. It's this little megaphone. Sometimes you'll need to click all tools and then you'll need to navigate a navigate over to advertising settings if it's not just by default there."
[55:43]
"And once you're here, like down here towards this left-hand area, you're going to see a little option that pops up um talks about creative features. You see this creative features fucking You click here and then you want to make sure that this box is unchecked."
[55:59]
"I want to be very directed in saying they will still fucking you and they will still test things even if this is unchecked. Generally, even after you have that unchecked, you want to make sure that you look at this list."
[56:12]
"This list generally shows you the random AI generation features that they have on and when they turned them on. And you can click edit tests and you can uncheck all of these manually here as well. I know this sounds ridiculous, but they're technically two separate things. You need to do both."
[56:29]
"And then on the ad level itself of a specific campaign, okay, when you go when you go look at the actual ad itself and and you look at the destination URL, this area here specifically, okay, right underneath this box is what I'm talking about."
[56:45]
"There'll be a little option that pops up that says like, let's see if I can get it to pop up here for you guys. I hope I I pray to God it doesn't show up. Um, okay. So, moral of the story, it will attempt to add random random, okay, URLs from your site that it can pull from. Okay? So, just be super mindful of that."
[57:07]
"In addition to all the related media being turned off, related media to be clear on the ad level. Um, when you actually go and you add your videos here, you see this section that says related media. This is also found on the adset level."
[57:21]
"So sometimes at the bottom of the actual ad set itself, you have this where it says suggested ads, make sure none of this fucking is turned on. But on the actual ad level itself, like when you look at the ad level, just make sure that this says zero, okay?"
[57:37]
"Because otherwise what it does is it just grabs random videos or random images. Okay? It's very problematic. That make sense? Okay, cool."
[57:47]
"All right. Devin says, uh, that true classic post. Do you see that? Yeah, with the grandma. I did. I did see that. Um, you're seeing bot traffic impact campaigns even after turning off audience network ad placements. Yes. Yes. It is not isolated to just audience network placements."

📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
Cliente webinar: $25,000-$75,000 por webinar
Cost per lead: $15-$25
Milhares de leads por webinar
Dezenas de vendas por webinar
6 meses de operação semanal
1/3 dos compradores assistem 2+ webinars antes de comprar
Brezen Pierre: $1M+/mês com weekly owner calls adicionando low six figures mensais
Daniel Fazio (List Kit): $1M+/mês
Show rates: David 70%, nome de 10 sílabas 40%, mudança para John 60%
Grant Cardone email: 40,000+ respostas em 24h de centenas de milhares enviados
Inner Circle: $10K/mês (completo) vs $5K/mês (Half Circle)
iOS 17 filtros impactando deliverability
Andromeda: 30% das campanhas com bot traffic issues, 5%+ CTR como red flag
Meta AI bug: CPMs +600%, spend +500%, 0.06 ROAS em Reels
Q1 Mastermind: Janeiro 17-18
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
MINDSET FUNDAMENTAL:
Conversion mechanism failed, não o lead
Factory conveyor belt analogy - não ignorar 50% que não passa
Interest Spectrum: Curiosity → General Interest → Highly Interested → Convicted
Interest cai com o tempo, só sobe com intervenções
Richard Bandler VAK System: rotate communication até funcionar
Otimizar para outcomes, não time and effort
LITTLE THINGS (Systematize):
Scheduling optimization (horários off-hours: 6 PM-11 PM, manhã cedo)
Setter teams para re-engagement
Marketing automation sequences
Backend selling systems (5 systems)
Trust & sales assets (Gamma docs, testimonials)
Salesperson name simplification
Webinar timing (opt-ins mais próximos = maior show rate)
BIG THINGS (Systematize):
Weekly Owner Cleanup Calls (1-2x/semana, diferentes dias/horários)
Alternative conversion mechanisms (webinars se tem calls, calls se tem webinars)
Multiple offer structures (diferentes price points, diferentes inclusões)
Cleaner sales role (2 semanas-1 mês post-ghost)
Low ticket/drops
In-person events
3-day virtual events
Alternative funnels para backend
BREAK THE GLASS TACTICS (Use durante slumps):
"For The Dogs" email sequence (feriados, períodos low-activity)
"Sent From iPhone" email (subject: "following up", one-liner, dash name, sent from iPhone footer)
"Where Did We Lose You" email (2 sentences, gera feedback ou revive lead)
Preferred Communication Channel blast (hit all channels)
ESTRUTURA:
Owner Call Flow: Presentation → Pitch → Questions → Re-pitch → Questions
Templates Disponíveis:
Mini Webinar 2.0 (~23 slides, make money offers)
Mini Webinar 1.0 (~26 slides, generalized)
VSSL framework
DSL (Dex Sales Letter)
Sales Organization:
Main closers (front-end)
Cleaner role (backend, 2 weeks-1 month post-ghost)
Setter teams
Owner as authority closer
Multi-Channel Approach:
Email (personal vs business, CRM awareness)
Text (iOS 17 complications)
Instagram DMs
Facebook
LinkedIn (platform-specific preferences)
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Q4: custos sobem, margens apertam
Backend cleanup = low six figures adicional/mês (Brezen case)
Multiple offers = mais conversões (Daniel Fazio)
3-day virtual events = couple million each (Cody Sanchez)
Leaky bucket: small % captured vs massive waste
Leads mortos quando interest = zero (custo de re-acquisition)
TECH ISSUES Q4 2024:
Andromeda bot traffic (30% occurrence rate)
Click Cease tool recomendado
Meta AI creative tools "dog shit phase"
Related media auto-pulling wrong content
Dynamic destination URLs
Site links manipulation
Facebook Reels placement bug oficial
iOS 17 hidden filters e voicemail issues
CASE STUDIES:
Brezen Pierre: Weekly owner calls → $1M+/mês
Daniel Fazio (List Kit): Multiple offers + off-hours scheduling → $1M+/mês
Cody Sanchez: Multi-funnel, multi-offer, in-person + virtual events
Grant Cardone: Sent from iPhone email → 40K responses, 48h company shutdown
DURAÇÃO: ~59 minutos FORMATO: Masterclass/Training interno com Q&A
